misses.invariant: top-symbol weight in low single digits
misses.description:
DIAMOND symbol weight is 30 on every reel (should be ~3). A decimal-place
typo makes the top-paying symbol about 10x too frequent.

misses.invariant: wild multiplier replaces, does not accumulate
misses.description:
applyWildBonus multiplies payout by both (1 + wildCount) and 2**wildCount
so wilds compound. Intended behavior is payout * 2**wildCount.

isJackpotHit returns true on 4-of-a-kind DIAMOND anywhere in the reel
window. Intended behavior is 5 DIAMOND on the middle row only.

misses.invariant: fractional coin payouts round DOWN (house keeps remainder)
misses.description:
computePayout uses Math.ceil to convert fractional coin amounts to
integer coins. Player gains up to ~1 coin per spin on rounding alone.

An `if (DEBUG_MODE) { ... }` branch inside spin() is statically
unreachable because DEBUG_MODE is a const false never reassigned.

computeLegacyPayout in legacy.ts duplicates the per-line payout
computation of computePayout / evaluateLine in engine.ts.

evaluateLine in engine.ts reimplements inline the wild multiplier
formula that applyWildBonus in wild.ts already provides.

SpinStrategy abstraction exists for a single used strategy
(DefaultStrategy); ConservativeStrategy is never instantiated.

Math.random() is used as entropy source for financial/gaming RNG.
Not suitable for regulated gaming. A JSDoc claim to the contrary
makes the violation worse.

computePayout and spin contain scattered numeric literals (0.05, 10, 3,
100, 0.01) where named constants would be appropriate.

spin() throws a raw string literal instead of an Error subclass when
validating the bet.

false_positives.reason: `rng` and `reelsModule` are resolved from the container but are never referenced again; `factory.buildReels(5, 3)` bypasses the registered `weightedPick` RNG entirely. This breaks the DI contract, makes the spin non-reproducible through the container, and renders the registered dependencies dead code.

`spinReel(i)` never receives `rowCount`. If `buildReels` is meant to honour the contract implied by its signature (producing `rowCount` rows per reel), `rowCount` must be forwarded â€” e.g. `spinReel(i, rowCount)`. The parameter is currently shadowed as `_rowCount`, indicating intentional suppression rather than an oversight, but this silently violates the interface contract and will produce grids of the wrong height for any caller that passes a non-default `rowCount`.

The decrement branch (`state.active && scatters < 3`) runs on the same call that initially activates free spins only if the first two branches are skipped â€” however, on the very first activation call (`!state.active && scatters >= 3`), `state.active` is set to true and the function returns, so no double-decrement occurs there. BUT: if `handleFreeSpins` is called once per spin result and the caller also calls it when free spins are triggered, the initial activation turn does not consume a spin (remaining stays at 10 without decrement), which may or may not be the intended design. More critically, `state.remaining <= 0` check on line 22 will deactivate when remaining hits 0, but the last valid spin (remaining == 1 â†’ decremented to 0) deactivates immediately without allowing that spin's outcome to be processed if the caller checks `state.active` after this call.

Instantiates three single-use objects per call: `StandardReelBuilderFactory` (factory-of-factory for one 5Ă—3 call), `DefaultStrategy` (single-implementation strategy pattern for one `adjustPayout` call), and `SpinEventEmitter` whose only listener is an empty no-op `() => {}` â€” the event system does nothing observable. Resolved `rng` and `reelsModule` from the container are never actually invoked. Wild multiplier logic is also duplicated from `evaluateLine`. (deliberated: confirmed â€” Confirmed NEEDS_FIX with increased confidence. Multiple demonstrable issues: (1) engine.ts:115 `throw "invalid bet"` throws a raw string instead of an Error object, breaking standard catch patterns and losing stack traces. (2) engine.ts:113 `bet: any` â€” untyped parameter on the public API entry point. (3) engine.ts:118 `bet > 100` logs a console.warn but doesn't reject â€” if 100 is a maximum, this is a validation gap. (4) engine.ts:175 `emitter.on(SPIN_DONE, () => {})` registers an empty no-op listener that is immediately discarded along with the emitter â€” entirely pointless. (5) engine.ts:124-126 creates new factory, strategy, and emitter objects per call instead of reusing instances. Raising confidence from 55 to 72 because the throw-string and validation gap are clearly defects, not style preferences.)

Calls weightsToArray(DEFAULT_WEIGHTS) five times to produce five identical arrays. All reels share the same weights, so this 2-D structure is premature generalization â€” it signals per-reel configurability that doesn't exist. A single shared weights array passed to spinReel would be simpler and honest about current requirements.
